criteria.issue_identification: Accuracy of found issues — catches real bugs, logic errors, convention violations (1=misses obvious problems, 5=catches subtle issues)
criteria.actionability: Suggestions are concrete with examples or specific fix descriptions (1=vague hand-waving, 5=specific with example code)
criteria.false_positives: Noise level — flagged issues are real problems (1=mostly noise, 5=all findings are genuine issues)
criteria.coverage: Proportion of changed code actually reviewed and analyzed (1=only glanced at diff, 5=comprehensive including context)
criteria.severity_calibration: Issue priorities match actual impact — HIGH for bugs, LOW for nits (1=everything marked critical, 5=well-calibrated severity)
